title: Lantern Metropolis Tower
description: A narrow inhabited tower designed for dense procedural instancing across the lantern basin. Its body is a dark tapered civic column wrapped by irregular balconies, broken window bands, suspended lantern cages, and a luminous crown. The asset supports many deterministic variants while retaining one unmistakable family silhouette.
narrativeFunction: The tower turns the basin into a believable city rather than a field of lights. Repetition supplies urban density and scale, while balcony, window, and crown variation prevents procedural sameness. Each tower acts as a small vertical archive where domestic memory and civic ritual overlap.

orientation.originMeaning: The origin is centered on the tower footprint at ground level, enabling radial city placement, deterministic height scaling, bridge attachment, and vertical LOD substitution.

silhouette.primary: A slim tower tapering inward through the middle and opening into a wider luminous crown, producing a readable needle-and-lantern profile.
silhouette.secondary: Three to five offset balcony plates alternate around the shaft and create asymmetry without exceeding the width of the crown cage.
silhouette.tertiary: Window slits, hanging cages, aerial-bridge sockets, vents, maintenance ladders, roof fins, gutters, and repair seams create localized close-view detail.
silhouette.negativeSpace: Gaps below balconies, the open cage around the crown light, and occasional bridge sockets remain open. The shaft never becomes a uniformly solid glowing column.
silhouette.recognitionTest: At city scale every variant reads as the same family: a dark tapered shaft carrying a warm open lantern crown, even when total height, balcony order, and window pattern differ.

materials.surfaceDescription: Black-violet cast stone and metal composite with vertical rain streaks, repaired panel seams, soot accumulation, and slightly polished balcony edges.

materials.role: Balcony frames, crown cage, and bridge hardware
materials.surfaceDescription: Warm dark bronze with soot around lantern cages, rubbed rail tops, turquoise oxidation in protected corners, and small engraved civic marks.
Layered warm light with brighter cores, dimmer outer glass, and subtle hue variation suggesting many occupied rooms rather than one flat emissive panel.

components.detail: Use a faceted ten-sided shaft with three vertical service channels, one recessed entry seam, uneven panel breaks, and a slightly heavier lower third.

components.role: Thin inhabited window slits distributed unevenly along selected shaft facets.
components.detail: Window groups vary in length and wrap selected facets without forming continuous horizontal rings; at least one vertical region remains mostly dark.

components.role: Offset civic balconies and maintenance decks breaking the shaft silhouette.
components.detail: Every plate receives a thin rail, one visible support rib, one hanging cage or planter socket, and a unique angular projection around the shaft.

components.role: Open ring cage supporting the hero lantern at the tower top.
components.detail: The crown includes four vertical cage struts, one narrow roof fin, a hanging maintenance loop, and one deliberately absent quadrant for silhouette asymmetry.

components.role: Primary urban light source and tower-family identity mark.
components.detail: The light has a bright elongated center, dim outer glass, faint vertical flame motion, and a narrow shadow band cast by the incomplete cage.

assemblyOrder: Insert lantern core, maintenance details, and bridge sockets
deformationRules: Scale total height from seventy to one hundred thirty percent while preserving footprint and crown proportions.
Rotate each balcony around the shaft using a deterministic non-repeating sequence and vary projection by twelve centimeters.
Allow a maximum three-degree whole-tower lean toward the basin center for peripheral instances.
variationRules: Window occupancy varies independently by floor while at least twenty percent of windows remain dark.
Crown light hue shifts narrowly among amber, pale gold, and muted rose.
Balcony count ranges from three to five and neighboring towers may not share the same order.

topology.target: Game-ready instancing-friendly hard-surface model with one shaft mesh, detachable balcony modules, reusable window inserts, crown cage, and separate emissive lantern core.
topology.bevel: Use narrow architectural bevels on shell facets, wider touch-safe balcony and rail bevels, and soft crown-light glass edges.
topology.subdivision: Avoid subdivision on the faceted shaft. Use local smoothing only for crown light, hanging cages, and selected rail curves.
topology.normals: Weighted normals preserve the ten-sided tower facets and give balcony plates and crown hardware crisp but stable highlights.
topology.uv: Use one shared tower-shell trim region, one bronze hardware trim, packed window-emission strips, and a unique crown-light mask.

animation.motionCharacter: Urban light behavior is quiet and asynchronous. Crown lights breathe slowly, individual window groups change over long intervals, and hanging cages respond with minimal wind-driven sway.
constraints: No more than fifteen percent of windows change state within one second.
The crown light never fully extinguishes.

collision.description: Use one tapered capsule or low-sided convex hull for the shaft. Balconies, windows, bridge sockets, cages, and crown structure remain decorative.

sockets.purpose: Terrain or terrace placement at tower footprint.
Optional pedestrian bridge connection at lower-mid elevation.
Optional bridge connection on an opposing upper facet.
Lantern sparks, fog illumination, or city locator effect.

orthographicGuidance.front: Show tapered shaft, irregular balcony projections, broken warm window bands, and the crown cage widening beyond the upper shaft.
orthographicGuidance.side: Reveal balcony rotation, one aerial bridge socket, crown depth, and the slight inward taper through the middle.
orthographicGuidance.top: Display the faceted ten-sided shaft, asymmetric balcony footprints, incomplete crown ring, and bridge connection directions.
orthographicGuidance.threeQuarter: Use a low upward angle with a darker neighboring tower silhouette for scale; inhabited windows remain visible without overexposing the shell.
orthographicGuidance.scaleCue: Place a human on the largest balcony and another at the base entry; the tower reads as roughly three compact urban stories.

validation: The default tower reads as a tapered dark shaft with a wider open luminous crown.
At least three balconies project in different directions and remain physically supported.
Window bands occupy multiple facets and include deliberate dark gaps.
The crown cage contains an open quadrant and never becomes a closed cap.
Instanced height variation preserves the ground pivot and crown proportions.
LOD2 retains shaft taper, one balcony break, and the crown light.
Bridge sockets remain outside the shaft and use opposing outward orientations.
